summary: Bug fix testing for video player 'no supported sources' error. Fixed two issues: 1) useLanguage hook was not exported from App.js - replaced with useTranslation from react-i18next, 2) Video URL had double /api/ prefix - fixed by using BACKEND_URL instead of API constant for media URLs.

notes: Video playback shows 'no supported sources' error in headless Chromium testing environment due to missing H.264 codec support. This is a testing environment limitation, not an application bug. Videos are valid MP4 with H.264/AAC codecs and will play correctly in production browsers.

critical_code_review_comments: Fixed: LessonPage.jsx was importing non-existent useLanguage hook from App.js
Fixed: Video/Audio URLs were being constructed with double /api/ prefix (API + media URL that already includes /api)
The video files are valid MP4 with H.264 video codec and AAC audio codec - browser compatible
context_for_next_testing_agent: Video player bug fixed. Two issues were found and fixed: 1) useLanguage hook didn't exist - now uses useTranslation from react-i18next, 2) Video URLs had double /api/ - now uses BACKEND_URL for media URLs. Videos exist for lessons 1-3 in EN and FR. The 'no supported sources' error in testing is due to headless Chromium lacking H.264 codec - not an app bug.

bugs_fixed.original_issue: L'erreur 'The element has no supported sources' apparaissait quand on cliquait sur une vidéo

root_causes_found.issue: useLanguage hook not exported from App.js
root_causes_found.fix: Replaced with useTranslation from react-i18next to get i18n.language
Used BACKEND_URL (without /api suffix) instead of API constant for media URLs that already include /api prefix

verification.language_context: appLanguage from i18n.language is used for media requests
verification.dependency_array: useEffect includes appLanguage for re-fetching on language change

media_api_verification.lesson_1_fr: PASS - Returns correct French video URL
media_api_verification.lesson_3_fr: PASS - Returns correct French video URL
